name: Sendero Cascada de la Salceda - from Quisicedo — circular by LIC (Lugar Interés Comunitario) Ojo Guareña
name_original: Sendero Cascada de la Salceda - desde Quisicedo — circular por LIC (Lugar Interés Comunitario)  Ojo Guareña
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Lic Ojo Guareña traverse a unique karst landscape, defined by extensive limestone massifs and rivers that have sculpted impressive formations. The region features diverse vegetation, including meadows, grasslands, and Iberian forests with oak and beech, providing varied scenery. Hikers can explore natural arches like "El Ventanón" and the picturesque La Mea Waterfall, which cascades through a narrow ravine. This area offers a blend of surface trails and historical attractions, making it suitable for various outdoor activities. Best easy hiking trails in Lic Ojo Guareña The most popular easy hiking route is Torre de los Velasco – Santa Cecilia Church loop from Espinosa de los Monteros, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 10 minutes to complete. This route offers gentle gradients through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Trail Junction – The Ventanón loop from Villamartín de Sotoscueva, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) path. This trail leads to the impressive natural arch known as El Ventanón. Local hikers also love the Centennial Oak – Torre de los Velasco loop from Espinosa de los Monteros, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Hiking in Lic Ojo Guareña is defined by its unique karst system, diverse forests, and notable natural arches. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 29 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Lic Ojo Guareña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Lic Ojo Guareña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Lic Ojo Guareña offers a good selection of easy hiking trails, with 29 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, there are over 120 hiking routes across various difficulty levels in the region.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on easy hikes in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: The region is famous for its unique karst landscape, featuring impressive limestone massifs and rivers that have sculpted extensive underground systems. On easy trails, you can expect to see diverse vegetation, including meadows, grasslands, and Iberian forests with oak and beech. Notable natural features include natural arches like El Ventanón and the picturesque La Mea Waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails in Lic Ojo Guareña are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de los Velasco – Santa Cecilia Church loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail Junction – The Ventanón loop from Villamartín de Sotoscueva&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you to a spectacular natural arch.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see along easy hiking trails?
A: Along easy trails, you can discover a blend of natural and historical attractions. The area is known for its extensive cave system, and you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3900706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Bernabé of Ojo Guareña&lt;/a&gt;, which is built into a cave. Other points of interest include the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quisicedo&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3482674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cuerno&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Lic Ojo Guareña suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are well-suited for families. They generally feature gentle gradients and manageable distances. The diverse scenery, including forests and natural arches, provides an engaging experience for children. Consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860344" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cecilia Church loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What is the typical duration and distance for easy hikes in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Easy hikes in Lic Ojo Guareña typically range from 4 to 7 kilometers (2.5 to 4.3 miles) in distance. Most can be completed within 1 to 2 hours, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centennial Oak – Torre de los Velasco loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt; is about 4 km and takes just over an hour.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: The easy trails in Lic Ojo Guareña are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the unique karst formations, and the peaceful atmosphere of the diverse forests and meadows.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to a waterfall?
A: While specific easy routes directly to the La Mea Waterfall are not listed in the top easy trails, the region research indicates that the picturesque La Mea Waterfall cascades through a narrow ravine and is a notable natural feature. You might find trails in the broader area that offer views or access to waterfalls, often requiring a slightly longer walk.

Q: What is the best time of year to go on an easy hike in Lic Ojo Guareña?
A: Lic Ojo Guareña can be enjoyed throughout much of the year due to its varied climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal for hiking. Summer is also suitable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter can provide a unique, tranquil experience, though some paths might be wet or muddy.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several easy trails provide excellent viewpoints. The region's karst landscape often creates elevated spots with expansive vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3482674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Cuerno&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that offers panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and mountains, and there are easy paths that ascend to similar scenic points.

Q: Can I find easy trails that are less crowded or considered 'hidden gems'?
A: While the main attractions can draw visitors, Lic Ojo Guareña has a wide network of trails, and many easy routes offer a sense of tranquility. Exploring some of the less-advertised loops or trails connecting smaller villages, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31854828" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cecilia Church – Casa de Porras loop from Espinosa de los Monteros&lt;/a&gt;, can lead you to quieter spots where you can enjoy the natural beauty without large crowds.
tips: Pico del Angel is a mountain that is at the top of Quisicedo and where there is a picnic area where you can rest and enjoy this area.

This impressive 30-meter waterfall, formed by the waters of the San Miguel stream, remains hidden in the Sotoscueva Valley and is the culmination of a set of waterfalls that plunge down the slopes of Los Montes de Somo.
In the town of Quisicedo, which is followed by a set of small nuclei, there is the Iglesia de Santiago Apóstol in the old middle of the town.
